action.reasoning: Observation: Best test balanced accuracy is 0.8346 with best_s1.pt. The sweep showed logit adjustment and tau-normalization don't help (balanced softmax already handles class priors). The iter 8 training with extra regularization (drop_path, label_smoothing, mixup) achieved val 0.8330 which hasn't been tested on the test set yet. Analysis: Two opportunities: (1) Evaluate best.pt from iter 8 (might be better on test since val was 0.8330 > 0.8260 for best_s1.pt's val), (2) Train a ViT-B/16 CLIP model which could have better features for tail classes and enable future ensembling. Decision: Submit eval with best.pt AND start ViT training simultaneously. Update predict.py to auto-detect model type from checkpoint.
action.command: python -c "import torch; import timm; m = timm.create_model('convnext_tiny.fb_in22k_ft_in1k', pretrained=False, num_classes=100); ckpt = torch.load('checkpoints/best.pt', map_location='cpu', weights_only=False); m.load_state_dict(ckpt['ema_state_dict']); print('best.pt EMA loading OK')" && python train_vit.py
